---
title: "Data Visualization"
description: "Interactive charts and plots powered by Observable JS"
---

This page demonstrates interactive data visualizations built with Observable JS, which runs entirely in the browser — no server required.

---

## Interactive Bar Chart

Use the controls below to customize the chart:

```{ojs}
viewof numBars = Inputs.range([3, 20], {
  label: "Number of bars",
  step: 1,
  value: 8
})

viewof colorScheme = Inputs.select(
  ["Blues", "Greens", "Reds", "Purples", "Oranges"],
  { label: "Color scheme", value: "Blues" }
)
```

```{ojs}
barData = Array.from({ length: numBars }, (_, i) => ({
  label: `Item ${i + 1}`,
  value: Math.round(20 + Math.random() * 80)
}))
```

```{ojs}
// Use Plot from Observable
Plot.plot({
  title: "Interactive Bar Chart",
  marginLeft: 60,
  x: { label: "Value →" },
  y: { label: "Category" },
  color: { scheme: colorScheme },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(barData, {
      x: "value",
      y: "label",
      fill: "value",
      sort: { y: "-x" },
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

---

## Scatter Plot with Filtering

Explore a generated dataset with dynamic filtering:

```{ojs}
viewof sampleSize = Inputs.range([10, 500], {
  label: "Sample size",
  step: 10,
  value: 100
})

viewof showTrendline = Inputs.toggle({ label: "Show trend line", value: true })
```

```{ojs}
scatterData = Array.from({ length: sampleSize }, (_, i) => {
  const x = Math.random() * 100;
  const noise = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 30;
  return {
    x: x,
    y: 0.8 * x + 20 + noise,
    group: x < 33 ? "Low" : x < 66 ? "Medium" : "High"
  };
})
```

```{ojs}
scatterMarks = [
  Plot.dot(scatterData, {
    x: "x",
    y: "y",
    fill: "group",
    opacity: 0.7,
    r: 4,
    tip: true
  })
]

trendlineMarks = showTrendline
  ? [Plot.linearRegressionY(scatterData, { x: "x", y: "y", stroke: "red", strokeWidth: 2 })]
  : []

Plot.plot({
  title: "Scatter Plot with Grouping",
  color: { legend: true, scheme: "Set2" },
  grid: true,
  marks: [...scatterMarks, ...trendlineMarks]
})
```

---

## Time Series Chart

A dynamic time series with configurable parameters:

```{ojs}
viewof timePoints = Inputs.range([20, 200], {
  label: "Data points",
  step: 10,
  value: 60
})

viewof smoothing = Inputs.range([0, 10], {
  label: "Smoothing window",
  step: 1,
  value: 3
})
```

```{ojs}
timeData = (() => {
  const data = [];
  let value = 50;
  const start = new Date("2023-01-01");
  for (let i = 0; i < timePoints; i++) {
    value += (Math.random() - 0.48) * 5;
    value = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, value));
    const date = new Date(start);
    date.setDate(start.getDate() + i);
    data.push({ date, value: Math.round(value * 100) / 100 });
  }
  return data;
})()
```

```{ojs}
smoothedData = timeData.map((d, i) => {
  const window = timeData.slice(
    Math.max(0, i - smoothing),
    Math.min(timeData.length, i + smoothing + 1)
  );
  const avg = window.reduce((s, w) => s + w.value, 0) / window.length;
  return { ...d, smoothed: Math.round(avg * 100) / 100 };
})

Plot.plot({
  title: "Time Series with Optional Smoothing",
  x: { type: "time", label: "Date" },
  y: { label: "Value", domain: [0, 100] },
  marks: [
    Plot.line(smoothedData, { x: "date", y: "value", stroke: "#ccc", strokeWidth: 1 }),
    Plot.line(smoothedData, { x: "date", y: "smoothed", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.areaY(smoothedData, {
      x: "date",
      y: "smoothed",
      fill: "steelblue",
      fillOpacity: 0.1
    })
  ]
})
```

---

## Summary Statistics

Select a dataset property to see live summary statistics:

```{ojs}
viewof statSampleSize = Inputs.range([30, 1000], {
  label: "Sample size",
  step: 10,
  value: 200
})

viewof distribution = Inputs.select(
  ["Normal", "Uniform", "Exponential"],
  { label: "Distribution" }
)
```

```{ojs}
statData = (() => {
  const n = statSampleSize;
  if (distribution === "Normal") {
    // Box-Muller transform
    return Array.from({ length: n }, () => {
      const u1 = Math.random(), u2 = Math.random();
      return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u1)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * u2) * 15 + 50;
    });
  } else if (distribution === "Uniform") {
    return Array.from({ length: n }, () => Math.random() * 100);
  } else {
    return Array.from({ length: n }, () => -Math.log(Math.random()) * 20);
  }
})()

statValues = {
  const sorted = [...statData].sort((a, b) => a - b);
  const mean = statData.reduce((s, v) => s + v, 0) / statData.length;
  const variance = statData.reduce((s, v) => s + (v - mean) ** 2, 0) / statData.length;
  const median = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length / 2)];
  const q1 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.25)];
  const q3 = sorted[Math.floor(sorted.length * 0.75)];
  return {
    mean: mean.toFixed(2),
    median: median.toFixed(2),
    std: Math.sqrt(variance).toFixed(2),
    min: Math.min(...statData).toFixed(2),
    max: Math.max(...statData).toFixed(2),
    q1: q1.toFixed(2),
    q3: q3.toFixed(2)
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
html`<div class="stats-grid">
  ${Object.entries(statValues).map(([k, v]) =>
    `<div class="stat-card">
      <div class="stat-label">${k.toUpperCase()}</div>
      <div class="stat-value">${v}</div>
    </div>`
  ).join('')}
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
Plot.plot({
  title: `${distribution} Distribution (n = ${statSampleSize})`,
  marks: [
    Plot.rectY(statData, Plot.binX({ y: "count" }, { x: d => d, fill: "steelblue", tip: true })),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```
